name: Valkar Academy
description:
🏅 Rank Categories:

Rank S – Elite

Top 1–3%.

Access to highest-tier materials, elite classes, personal mentors, and best dorms.

Often given research projects and leadership roles.

Rank A – Advanced

Top 10–15%.

Access to rare materials, advanced training, and special missions.

Strong in combat and theory.

Rank B – Competent

Above-average.

Standard classes, access to uncommon materials, and group training.

Reliable, improving.

Rank C – Developing

Basic proficiency.

Placed in foundational classes with limited resources.

Focus on control and knowledge.

Rank D – Remedial

Below average.

Assigned to correctional programs and strict monitoring.

Minimal access to tools.

Rank X – Special / Restricted

Unstable, overwhelming, or ultra-rare magic types.

Requires isolation, special materials, and constant surveillance.

🛠️ Resources:

🔷 Rank S – Tier 0 (Mythic/Restricted)

Starcore – Dense, radiant material formed under extreme magical pressure. Boosts energy-based spells, amplifies power output. Volatile.

Dragon’s Blood – High-mana biological compound. Greatly enhances spell potency and regeneration. Use must be regulated to prevent overload or mutation.

Ashiron Steel – Metal refined with pure heat magic. Used for weapon enhancement and high-end spell-conductive equipment.

Phoenix Resin – Rejuvenating substance used to stabilize dying spells or restore broken enchantments. Rare and one-use only.

Ancient Bone Dust – Crushed remains of extinct magical creatures. Used in necromancy, durability enchantments, or memory-binding spells.

⚔️ Rank A – Tier 1 (Rare)

Moonroot – A low-light plant that stores ambient energy. Used in stealth, illusion, and concealment spells.

Storm Crystal – Mineral formed during magical storms. Stores kinetic charge, used in lightning or barrier spells.

Griffin Horn Shavings – Used in speed and aerial enhancement spells. Improves reaction time when brewed into potions.

Spirit Ash – Residue from sacred cremation rituals. Used in spiritual detection, barrier reinforcement, and binding.

Ember Pearl – Heat-compressed stone used to maintain internal heat in enchanted items or clothing.

🔮 Rank B – Tier 2 (Uncommon)

Ironbark Slivers – Extremely dense wood with natural resistance to magic. Used in armor, wands, and durable constructs.

Silvervine Sap – A sticky, mana-conductive liquid used in rituals or rune carving.

Coalglass – Volcanic glass cooled by water magic. Stores thermal energy. Used in combustion and forge enchantments.

Arcane Sand – Finely ground crystal powder used for drawing temporary glyphs or wards.

Nightmetal Filings – Magnetically sensitive metal used in motion or levitation spells.

📘 Rank C – Tier 3 (Common)

Glowmoss Spores – Emits faint light when activated by mana. Used in practice potions and light runes.

Binding Thread – Fiber infused with mild enchantments. Holds wards, secures spell components.

Runestone Chips – Broken pieces of lesser runes. Good for inscription and energy flow exercises.

Aether Ink (Standard Grade) – Used to write spells on parchment or draw magical diagrams.

Cooling Powder – Low-level elemental dust used in temperature adjustment spells.

🧱 Rank D – Tier 4 (Basic/Training)

Chalk Dust – Used to draw low-energy runes or train precision casting.

Mana Clay – Shaped into practice charms or talismans. Only holds basic enchantments.

Focusing Beads – Helps young casters stabilize spellcasting. Worn or embedded in tools.

Training Salts – Used in low-level potion brewing or elemental control drills.

Neutral Wax – For sealing scrolls, charms, or containing small spell charges.

❗ Rank X – High-Control, High-Power Materials (Restricted Use)

Refined Dragon’s Blood – Distilled to purest form. Unlocks high-tier elemental magic or strengthens physical spells. Danger
